# hosting-pages publishing contract

This repository is an aggregation and publishing repository for CI reports
from multiple source repositories.

Source repositories should keep their GitHub Actions simple:
they may push generated Playwright, VRT, Storybook, or other static report
outputs into this repository. This repository is responsible for deciding
what is published to GitHub Pages, what is kept only as raw artifacts, and
what is cleaned up.

## Source repository responsibilities

A source repository may deploy a report by cloning this repository, replacing
one report directory, committing, rebasing, and pushing to main.

Recommended shape:

- Playwright report:
  - `<repo-slug>/<pull-request-number>/`
  - Example: `yps-crispy-carnival/528/`

- VRT report:
  - `<repo-slug>-vrt/pr-<pull-request-number>/`
  - Example: `yps-crispy-carnival-vrt/pr-533/`

- VRT baseline:
  - `<repo-slug>-vrt-baselines/<base-ref>/`
  - Example: `yps-crispy-carnival-vrt-baselines/develop/`

The source repository should remove and replace only its target directory.
It should not create per-PR branches in this repository.

## Recommended GitHub Actions push pattern

Use a token that can push to `yn1323/hosting-pages`.

```sh
git clone --depth 1 --single-branch \
  "https://x-access-token:${HOSTING_PAGES_TOKEN}@github.com/yn1323/hosting-pages.git" \
  hosting-pages

rm -rf "hosting-pages/${REPORT_DIR}/${REPORT_KEY}"
mkdir -p "hosting-pages/${REPORT_DIR}/${REPORT_KEY}"
cp -R "${LOCAL_REPORT_DIR}/." "hosting-pages/${REPORT_DIR}/${REPORT_KEY}/"

cd hosting-pages
git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
git add .
git commit -m "${REPORT_COMMIT_MESSAGE}" || exit 0
git pull --rebase
git push origin HEAD:main
```

For Playwright pull request reports:

```sh
REPORT_DIR="yps-crispy-carnival"
REPORT_KEY="${PR_NUMBER}"
LOCAL_REPORT_DIR="playwright-report"
REPORT_COMMIT_MESSAGE="Playwright report for PR #${PR_NUMBER}"
```

For VRT pull request reports:

```sh
REPORT_DIR="yps-crispy-carnival-vrt"
REPORT_KEY="pr-${PR_NUMBER}"
LOCAL_REPORT_DIR="vrt-work/reg"
REPORT_COMMIT_MESSAGE="VRT report for PR #${PR_NUMBER}"
```

For VRT branch reports:

```sh
REPORT_DIR="yps-crispy-carnival-vrt"
REPORT_KEY="branches/${BRANCH_SLUG}"
LOCAL_REPORT_DIR="vrt-work/reg"
REPORT_COMMIT_MESSAGE="VRT report for ${BRANCH_NAME}"
```

## What source repositories should not do

Source repositories should not try to optimize GitHub Pages payload size.
They should not remove videos, traces, passed screenshots, or raw report files
just for Pages performance.

Those publishing decisions belong to this repository.

Source repositories should also not create separate publishing branches for
each PR. GitHub Pages publishes one site for this repository, and per-PR
results should be represented as directories on `main`.

## hosting-pages responsibilities

This repository should:

- build a `_site` directory for GitHub Pages from the current tracked files;
- keep Pages payload lightweight;
- exclude or relocate heavy raw artifacts such as `.webm`, trace `.zip`, and
  large Playwright trace directories from the Pages artifact;
- publish useful report entrypoints such as `index.html`, VRT `out.json`,
  VRT `diff/`, and required VRT `actual/expected` images;
- keep VRT baseline files available for future comparisons;
- clean old report directories on a scheduled basis;
- keep the Pages artifact below GitHub Pages limits.

The source repository URL contract should remain stable. For example:

- `https://yn1323.github.io/hosting-pages/yps-crispy-carnival/528/`
- `https://yn1323.github.io/hosting-pages/yps-crispy-carnival-vrt/pr-533/`

## Retention policy

Short-lived PR report directories should be cleaned after 7 days unless a
future workflow marks them as protected.

VRT baselines are not ordinary reports. They should be protected from normal
PR report cleanup because they are used as comparison inputs.

## Notes for future agents

When changing this repository, prefer centralizing report filtering here rather
than requiring every source repository to implement the same filtering rules.

Before changing cleanup or publishing behavior, inspect the actual current tree
size and the generated GitHub Pages artifact size. The main risk is allowing
large Playwright videos, trace zips, or full VRT passed-image sets to enter the
Pages artifact.
